name: Camino Natural del Río Barbantiño – circular from A Forxa
name_original: Camino Natural del Río Barbantiño – circular desde A Forxa
region_intro: Hiking around Punxín offers diverse landscapes within the Ourense province of Galicia, Spain. The region is characterized by lush, green environments, the Barbantiño River with its notable waterfall, and the significant Miño River. Thermal springs are also a prominent natural feature, providing opportunities for relaxation. These natural elements combine to create varied terrain suitable for outdoor activities. Best hiking trails around Punxín The most popular hiking route is Camino Natural del Río Barbantiño – circular from A Forxa, a 7.5 miles (12.1 km) trail that takes 3 hours 22 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows the Barbantiño River, offering views of its natural features. Another top favourite among local hikers is Route of the Fuente de San Benito – circular from Mugares, a moderate 10.4 miles (16.8 km) path. This route explores the local countryside and takes approximately 4 hours 51 minutes. Local hikers also love the Barbantes Thermal Park loop from Razamonde, a 6.9 miles (11.1 km) trail leading through the area surrounding the thermal park, often completed in about 3 hours 3 minutes. Hiking around Punxín is defined by the Barbantiño River, thermal springs, and lush green environments. The network offers options for both easy and moderate ability levels. The routes in Punxín are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 60 reviews. More than 300 hikers have used komoot to explore Punxín's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Punxín?
A: Punxín offers a good selection of routes for hikers, with over 20 trails available. These range from easy strolls to more moderate hikes, ensuring options for various fitness levels.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Punxín?
A: Yes, many of the trails around Punxín are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e976234765/camino-natural-del-rio-barbantino-circular-from-a-forxa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino Natural del Río Barbantiño – circular from A Forxa&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 7.5-mile route following the Barbantiño River.

Q: Can I find hikes that feature waterfalls or other natural water features?
A: Absolutely. The region is known for its lush, green environments and the Barbantiño River. You can explore the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5338813" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barbantiño Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, which is approximately 15 to 20 meters high. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24311709" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barbantiño Waterfall – Molinos de Pazos de Arriba loop from Punxín&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 5.4-mile route that takes you directly to this natural highlight.

Q: Are there any thermal springs or wild swimming spots near the hiking trails?
A: Punxín is close to several thermal complexes. After a hike, you can relax at the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3108574" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Outariz Hot Springs&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5017252" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Outariz and Burga de Canedo Thermal Baths&lt;/a&gt;, known for their therapeutic properties. For a natural swimming experience, the Barbantes Natural Pools along the Miño River offer a place to cool off.

Q: What historical landmarks can I explore while hiking in Punxín?
A: For those interested in history, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6474629" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Medieval Rock Tombs of Castro de Santa Mariña&lt;/a&gt; offer a glimpse into the region's past. This site is situated on a fort 326 meters above sea level, providing both historical interest and scenic views.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Punxín?
A: Yes, Punxín offers several easy and moderate routes suitable for families. The lush, green environments and natural features like the Barbantiño River provide an engaging backdrop for younger hikers. Look for shorter, less strenuous paths to ensure an enjoyable experience for everyone.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Punxín?
A: The region's lush, green scenery makes it appealing throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures for hiking. While specific seasonal considerations aren't detailed, Galicia's climate typically provides a refreshing backdrop for outdoor activities.

Q: Is parking available near the trailheads in Punxín?
A: While specific parking details for every trailhead are not provided, many hiking areas in rural Galicia, including around Punxín, typically have designated or informal parking spots near popular starting points. It's advisable to check route descriptions on komoot for specific parking recommendations for individual trails.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Punxín?
A: The trails in Punxín are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 60 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, the beauty of the Barbantiño River, and the overall lush, green environment that defines the region.

Q: Are there any easy hiking routes for beginners in Punxín?
A: Yes, Punxín has 7 easy routes perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed walk. These trails allow you to enjoy the natural beauty of the area without significant elevation changes or long distances. You can find options that explore the local countryside and riverbanks.

Q: Can I find places for refreshments or cafes near the hiking trails?
A: While specific cafes directly on every trail are not listed, the region around Punxín and nearby towns will offer opportunities for refreshments. The presence of thermal parks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5338823" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barbantes Thermal Park&lt;/a&gt; often means there are facilities nearby where you can find food and drinks.

Q: Are there any trails that offer particularly good views?
A: Many trails in Punxín offer scenic views, especially those along the Barbantiño and Miño Rivers, or routes that ascend to higher points. For instance, the location of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6474629" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Medieval Rock Tombs of Castro de Santa Mariña&lt;/a&gt; provides excellent vantage points over the surrounding landscape.

Q: How can I reach the hiking areas using public transport?
A: Public transport options to specific trailheads in rural areas like Punxín can be limited. While Ourense province has some bus services, direct access to all hiking starting points might require a car. It's recommended to check local bus schedules for routes connecting to Punxín or nearby villages if you plan to rely on public transport.
tips:
In the Carballiño region, between the municipalities of Amoeiro and Maside, is the Barbantino River waterfall, also known as Fervenza do Cachón.
In times of heavy rains, this waterfall, 15 meters high, is a highly recommended visit to enjoy this natural spectacle.
Old mill located in a natural environment of great beauty, next to the Barbantiño river, where there are small bridges and footbridges, as well as other old mills.
